_title.ISO_639_3_eng: Quantities, Units, Dimensions and Types ontology

_definition.ISO_639_3_eng: Namespace for terms derived from the QUDT (Quantities, Units, Dimensions and Types) ontology.

_description.ISO_639_3_eng:
QUDT is an open ontology for scientific measurement: it defines quantity kinds, units of measure, unit conversion factors, and dimensional analysis using a formal RDF/OWL model. Published at [qudt.org](https://qudt.org) and maintained in the [qudt/qudt-public-repo](https://github.com/qudt/qudt-public-repo) GitHub repository.

This dictionary uses QUDT as an import source for unit URIs ([`_uri`](_uri.md)), conversion multipliers ([`QUDT_multiplier`](QUDT_multiplier.md)), conversion offsets ([`QUDT_offset`](QUDT_offset.md)), and dimension vectors ([`QUDT_dimension_vector`](QUDT_dimension_vector.md)). QUDT is not a live dependency — all values are copied into the term records at import time.

_definition.ISO_639_3_eng: Namespace for QUDT dimensional analysis terms.

_description.ISO_639_3_eng: QUDT represents the physical dimension of a quantity kind as a vector of signed integer exponents over eight basis dimensions: A (amount of substance), E (electric current), L (length), I (luminous intensity), M (mass), H (thermodynamic temperature), T (time), and D (dimensionless ratio). This namespace groups descriptor terms that describe those dimensional properties, sourced from QUDT's `qudt:dimensionVectorForSI` predicate.
